name: ux-researcher-designer
description: UX research and design toolkit for Senior UX Designer/Researcher including data-driven persona generation, journey mapping, usability testing frameworks, and research synthesis. Use for user research, persona creation, journey mapping, and design validation.
capabilities: Analyzes user behavior patterns from raw data to identify trends
Generates four persona archetypes: power user, casual user, business user, mobile-first
Extracts psychographics including motivations, values, and attitudes
Creates usage scenarios based on persona type and patterns
Calculates confidence scores based on sample size
Derives actionable design implications from persona data
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure Python utility for persona generation. All 55 static findings are false positives: Python random module flagged as weak crypto (benign PRNG for UI), GitHub URL in metadata misidentified as hardcoded secret, markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution. No network calls, no file system access beyond script execution, no external commands, no environment variable access. Behavior matches stated purpose.
valueStatement: Creating user personas from raw research data is time-consuming and often inconsistent. This toolkit analyzes user behavior patterns, demographics, and interview insights to automatically generate research-backed personas with confidence scoring and design implications.
userTitle: Generate user personas from research data
limitations: Requires structured user data input (age, usage frequency, device, pain points)
Does not conduct actual user interviews or surveys
Cannot validate personas against real user feedback without additional tools
Limited to four predefined archetype templates

useCases.title: Synthesize interview data
useCases.description: Convert raw user interview notes and survey responses into structured personas for stakeholder presentations.

useCases.title: Inform design decisions
useCases.description: Generate personas with design implications to guide feature prioritization and user flow decisions.

useCases.title: Validate user segments
useCases.description: Analyze user data to confirm or challenge assumptions about target user segments and their needs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic persona request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the UX Researcher toolkit to generate a persona from this user data: [paste user research data with age, usage patterns, pain points, and device preferences]

promptTemplates.title: Persona with interviews
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a detailed persona from this user data, including interview insights about goals and motivations: [user data + interview quotes and observations]

promptTemplates.title: Compare user segments
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the attached user research dataset and generate personas for the different user segments you identify, including confidence levels and design implications for each.

promptTemplates.title: Mobile user focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the UX Researcher toolkit to create a mobile-first persona based on mobile usage data and on-the-go context patterns from the research findings provided.

bestPractices: Provide at least 20 user data points for reliable persona generation and meaningful patterns
Include interview insights with quotes and motivations for richer psychographic detail
Validate generated personas with actual users before using them for design decisions
antiPatterns: Using personas based on fewer than 10 users or assumptions rather than research data
Treating personas as exact representations instead of useful abstractions
Creating only one persona when user research reveals multiple distinct user segments

faq.question: What data format does the persona generator accept?
faq.answer: JSON array of user objects with fields like age, usage_frequency, primary_device, usage_context, features_used, pain_points, and tech_proficiency.

faq.question: How many users do I need for accurate personas?
faq.answer: The tool works with any sample size, but confidence is rated High for 50+ users, Medium for 20-50, and Low for fewer than 20.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my existing research tools?
faq.answer: The Python script reads from stdin or file input, making it compatible with data exports from surveys, analytics tools, and CRM systems.

faq.question: Is user data stored or sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. The persona generator runs entirely locally in your environment and does not make any network calls or external connections.

faq.question: How is this different from manual persona creation?
faq.answer: The tool uses algorithmic pattern detection to identify trends across users, ensuring consistency and reducing bias compared to manual interpretation.

faq.question: What AI tools is this compatible with?
faq.answer: This toolkit works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It can be used standalone or invoked by AI assistants during research and design workflows.
